mnemonics.title: ISO 9126 Quality Characteristics
mnemonics.content: F R U E M P - Fruemp: Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability

mnemonics.title: McCall's Quality Factors
mnemonics.content: CREFT UP - Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Flexibility, Testability, Usability, Maintainability, Portability, Integrity, Interoperability, Reusability (some variations exist)

mnemonics.title: CMMI Maturity Levels
mnemonics.content: IREDO - Initial, Repeatable, Defined, Optimizing (Level 5 is Quantitatively Managed between Defined and Optimizing)

mnemonics.title: Defect Life Cycle States
mnemonics.content: N A O F V C - New, Assigned, Open, Fixed, Verified, Closed

acronyms.usage: Total investment to ensure quality: prevention + appraisal + failure costs
Reliability metric - average time between system failures
Maintainability metric - average time to fix a failure
Percentage of defects removed before product release
Product-oriented activities to detect and fix defects

visualTips.visualization: Draw a tree with 'Quality' as root, branches as main characteristics (ISO 9126 or McCall), and leaves as sub-characteristics. This hierarchical structure helps remember all factors.
Use an iceberg diagram - prevention/appraisal are visible small costs above water, failure costs are the massive hidden costs below water.
Draw a triangle with Unit tests at bottom (large base), Integration in middle, System/UI at top (small tip). Shows testing effort distribution.
Circular flow diagram showing states: New → Assigned → Open → Fixed → Verified → Closed → New (reopened)

Staircase diagram with 5 steps, each higher level building on the previous - Initial (chaotic) → Repeatable (processes defined) → Defined (managed) → Quantitatively Managed (measured) → Optimizing (continuously improving)

analogies.analogy: Like building a house - quality foundation, materials, and craftsmanship prevent costly repairs later. Skipping quality is like building with cracks that eventually collapse.
QA is like preventive medicine (checkups, healthy habits to prevent illness), QC is like diagnostic medicine (tests to find and treat problems).
Reliability is how rarely a car breaks down, maintainability is how quickly it can be fixed when it does break down.
Like insurance premiums - paying small amounts now (prevention/appraisal) prevents massive costs later (failures/claims).
